context: External stabilization session for agents-lab 0.8 readiness. The board is release-clear: no open P0, no in-progress tasks, and no blocked tasks. The default scheduler is now intentionally drained: only protected parked P3 external-influence tasks remain planned, and they are skipped unless the operator explicitly selects that research lane. The remaining v0.8.0 readiness blocker is deliberate versioning only: packages are still 0.7.0, so target-version-ready stays false until the operator explicitly chooses the bump/tag/release lane. Recent work tightened package runtime dependency boundaries, clarified stale colony readiness history, aligned governance docs and maintainer workflow to pnpm, closed bounded clarity research, and hardened scheduler policy so protected parked backlog cannot be pulled into normal work by accident.
next_actions: Do not bump/tag/publish v0.8.0 without explicit operator release decision.
Before any long Pi runtime, re-run pi:dev:pressure or the equivalent runtime pressure gate in the current environment.
Keep the remaining planned external-influence tasks parked unless the operator explicitly selects a research lane with --priority p3 --include-protected or equivalent intent.
If continuing local-safe work, add or decompose a bounded task before relying on scheduler:next; the default queue currently has no eligible task.
Prefer local-safe slices that improve packaging, docs contracts, engine boundaries, runtime pressure observability, or first-party Pi stack distribution.
blockers: target-version-ready: false by design until packages are intentionally bumped from 0.7.0 to 0.8.0
scheduler-default-queue: no eligible tasks because the only planned work is protected parked P3 external research
